reason_for_decision: The article meets the pass threshold with a clear search-versus-confirmation framing and a strong explanation of why night surveillance usually needs both radar and thermal layers.
strengths: The article keeps the mission question focused on early detection, stable tracking, and visual confirmation rather than a false winner-loser contest.
It explains thermal line-of-sight and contrast limits without weakening the night-surveillance value of thermal imaging.
The recommended architecture of radar cueing thermal optics is operationally strong and easy to apply.
summary_assessment: This article passes comfortably. It is coherent, non-promotional, and genuinely useful for teams planning nighttime security sensing stacks.
